title: [No title]

description: Holotypes of Papagomys theodorverhoeveni (above) and Papagomys armandvillei (below). Four diagnostic characteristics are marked: the anterocentral cusp (1), the anterolabial cusplet (2), the posterolabial cusplet (3), and the anterolabial cusp (4). [Description from source]

title: [No title]
creator: Ucucha
description: Left lower molar row of the extinct Martinique giant rat, Megalomys desmarestii. This is specimen RMNH 26124 in the collections of Naturalis. This image shows the commonly used terms for the three lower molars: m1, m2, and m3. [Description from source]

title: Figure 2
creator: E. Grace Veatch; Matthew W. Tocheri; Thomas Sutikna; Kate McGrath; E. Wahyu Saptomo; Jatmiko; Kristofer M. Helgen
description: Right mandibular molar rows (above) and corresponding images of rats (below) endemic to Flores that illustrate the five body size categories defined and used in this study. From left to right, Rattus hainaldi (a photograph of Rattus exulans is shown), Paulamys naso, Komodomys rintjanus, Hooijeromys nusatenggara, Spelaeomys florensis, Papagomys theodorverhoeveni, and Papagomys armandvillei. Images modified from Musser (1981) and Kitchener et al., 1991a, Kitchener et al., 1991b. Sizes of the rat images for Hooijeromys nusatenggara, Spelaeomys florensis, and Papagomys theodorverhoeveni are estimated based on their respective molar sizes. [Description from source]
